summary: Shahed loitering munitions continue to be reported over multiple Ukrainian localities, including Vyshorod, Obolon, Kurenyvka, and Kyiv. Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs over Sumy and Donetsk regions, while a Russian strike hit a fire‑point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced UAV components. The United States reportedly struck an empty oil tanker bound for Iran, highlighting broader geopolitical tensions.
key_points: Shahed attacks were reported from Vyshorod/Obolon, Kurenyvka to Lukiantsi/Shulavka, and Vinogradar to Sirets, indicating dispersed loitering‑munitions activity across central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67450).
Two Shahed missiles launched from Kurenyvka targeted Lukiantsi and Shulavka (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67427).
A Russian strike hit a fire‑point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced long‑range UAV components (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Sumy and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/63690).
The United States launched a missile at an empty oil tanker heading to an Iranian port (Source: @tass_agency/378692).
Ukraine’s President Zelensky said five Kalibr missiles, 33 Iskander missiles and 650 attack drones require thousands of foreign‑sourced components (Source: @UkraineNow/81633).
Temporary fuel‑sale limits (20 L per day) were imposed in the LNR (Source: @tass_agency/378686).

sections.executive_summary: Shahed loitering‑munitions attacks were reported over Vyshorod, Obolon, Kurenyvka, and Kyiv, showing a spread of Russian‑supplied strike assets. Russian tactical aviation used guided aerial bombs in Sumy and Donetsk, while a strike on a Dnipropetrovsk fire‑point facility targeted UAV production. The United States hit an empty oil tanker bound for Iran, underscoring expanding external involvement.

points: President Zelensky highlighted that five Kalibr missiles, 33 Iskander missiles and 650 attack drones each require hundreds to thousands of foreign components, emphasizing Ukraine’s reliance on external supply chains (Source: @UkraineNow/81633).
Ukrainian intelligence officer Budanov stated Zelensky ordered the war to end before winter, deeming the goal realistic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30602).
A fire‑point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced long‑range UAV components was struck by Russian forces (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
Ukrainian MP Fedienko proposed a public register of draft dodgers to increase psychological pressure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30617).
SBU arrested a former Dnipro‑region soldier suspected of collaborating with Russian intelligence after he gathered information on missile and drone movements (Source: @UkraineNow/81624).
The United States launched a missile at an empty oil tanker bound for Iran, a target linked to Russian‑aligned interests (Source: @tass_agency/378692).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Sumy and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/63690).
Two suspects in a robbery involving a descendant of Pushkin were detained; one used a pneumatic pistol (Source: @tass_agency/378661).
Temporary fuel‑sale restrictions (no more than 20 L per day) were introduced in the LNR (Source: @tass_agency/378686).
President Putin and Belarusian leader Lukashenko discussed further development of bilateral alliance via telephone (Source: @tass_agency/378665).
Shahed missiles were reported from Kurenyvka targeting Lukiantsi and Shulavka in the eastern sector (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67427).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs over Donetsk, indicating continued air‑strike pressure in the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/63690).
A fire‑point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, supporting UAV production for eastern operations, was struck (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
Ukrainian forces reported increased pressure on Kostyantynivka with Russian infantry attempting infiltration from the east (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547).
Shahed missiles were reported from the north targeting Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67459).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over Chernihiv region heading toward Berizna, Borzna, and Kipti (Source: @eRadarrua/95098).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on north‑eastern and eastern directions, warning of aerial weapon use near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
Ukrainian air defence video showed engagement of over 700 aerial threats across north, south, east and centre on 2 June 2026 (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
UAV groups from the Black Sea were reported heading toward Mykolaiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63669).
A Shahed missile was reported from Vinogradar toward Sirets in the south (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67451).
Russian tactical aviation warned of possible ballistic weapon use in the south (Source: @kpszsu/63710).
Ukrainian forces noted a Shahed launch from the north toward Kyiv, indicating cross‑front missile mobility (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67458).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs over Sumy and Donetsk (Source: @kpszsu/63690).
Ukrainian air defence reported over 700 aerial attack means used by Russia on 2 June 2026, with dozens of UAVs shot down (Source: @kpszsu/63699).
Threat warnings were issued for ballistic weapon use in both northern and southern directions (Source: @kpszsu/63685; @kpszsu/63710).
Ukrainian public groups reported a massive missile attack on Kyiv with poor air‑defence performance (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30603).

key_developments: Shahed loitering‑munitions were reported over Vyshorod/Obolon, indicating continued Russian aerial harassment of central Ukrainian districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67450).
Two Shahed missiles launched from Kurenyvka struck Lukiantsi and Shulavka, showing coordinated attacks from the east (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67427).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Sumy and Donetsk, expanding the use of precision strike assets (Source: @kpszsu/63690).
A Russian strike hit a fire‑point facility in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast that produced long‑range UAV components, targeting Ukraine’s drone production capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30613).
The United States launched a missile at an empty oil tanker bound for Iran, reflecting heightened geopolitical tension involving Russian interests (Source: @tass_agency/378692).
President Zelensky warned that key missile and drone systems depend on thousands of foreign‑sourced components, underscoring supply‑chain vulnerabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81633).
Temporary fuel‑sale limits (20 L per day) were imposed in the LNR, indicating logistical constraints within Russian‑controlled territories (Source: @tass_agency/378686).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying the use of Shahed loitering munitions across central and northern Ukraine while expanding guided aerial bomb deployments in the east and south. The strike on a Dnipropetrovsk UAV component facility aims to degrade Ukraine's drone production capacity. External actors, notably the United States, are engaging in actions that could further complicate the strategic environment, as seen in the missile strike on an Iranian‑bound tanker.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are emphasizing the need for foreign component supplies to sustain missile and drone operations, while also increasing air‑defence alerts across all fronts. Russian logistical measures, such as fuel restrictions in the LNR, suggest internal resource pressures. International diplomatic focus appears divided, with the United States taking direct kinetic action in the region.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed launch sites should prioritize Vyshorod, Obolon, Kurenyvka, and Vinogradar to anticipate future strike vectors. UAV activity from the Black Sea toward Mykolaiv and from northern Chernihiv corridors requires enhanced radar coverage. The vulnerability of UAV component production facilities in Dnipropetrovsk highlights a critical node for protective measures.
